payload: {"patientId":"PX-001","drug":"Losartan","recallId":"D-1455-2019","classification":"II","reason":"CGMP Deviations: impurity for N-Nitroso-N-methyl-4-aminobutyric acid (NMBA) detected in the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) used to manufacture finished products.","status":"Ongoing","sourceUrl":"https://api.fda.gov/drug/enforcement.json?search=recall_number:%22D-1455-2019%22"}

{"patientId":"PX-001","chars":522,"bulletin":"⚠️ Pharmacovigilance alert (patient PX-001).\nThe FDA reports an active recall of your medication Losartan: Teva recalled 2,924,000 tablets (recall D-1455-2019, Class II) after the impurity NMBA was detected in the active ingredient. Class II means moderate risk: temporary or reversible harm; serious harm is unlikely. Check the lot number on your package (for example 1169752A or GS017387). IMPORTANT: do not stop your treatment on your own; contact your doctor or pharmacist to confirm your lot and discuss alternatives."}

{"patientId":"PX-002","drug":"Metformin","recallId":"D-1349-2020","classification":"II","reason":"CGMP Deviations: FDA analysis detected N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA) impurity above the acceptable intake level","status":"Ongoing","sourceUrl":"https://api.fda.gov/drug/enforcement.json?search=recall_number:%22D-1349-2020%22"}

{"patientId":"PX-002","chars":449,"bulletin":"⚠️ Pharmacovigilance alert (patient PX-002).\nYour extended-release Metformin 750 mg (Amneal) has an active FDA recall: recall D-1349-2020, Class II, due to the impurity NDMA above the acceptable limit. It affects more than 50 lots (codes starting with AM18, AM19 and AM20). Do not stop taking your metformin without talking to your doctor first: stopping treatment can be riskier than the impurity. Take your bottle to the pharmacy to check the lot."}

{"patientId":"PX-004","drug":"Clonazepam","recallId":"D-0179-2025","classification":"I","reason":"Labeling: Label Error on Declared Strength; Some cartons were incorrectly labeled. The blister strips inside the product carton reflect the correct strength.","status":"Ongoing","sourceUrl":"https://api.fda.gov/drug/enforcement.json?search=recall_number:%22D-0179-2025%22"}

{"patientId":"PX-004","chars":560,"bulletin":"⚠️ Pharmacovigilance alert (patient PX-004).\nYou have TWO active recalls. 1) Clonazepam tablets 0.125 mg (Endo/PAR): recall D-0179-2025, Class I — the most serious — due to a labeling error: some cartons state a strength that is not the real one; the blister inside shows the correct strength (lot 550174101). Check carton and blister before your next dose. 2) Ranitidine 150 mg (Granules India): recall D-0632-2020, Class II, due to the NDMA impurity. Do not stop either medication on your own: call your doctor or pharmacist today with your packages at hand."}

{"patientId":"PX-005","drug":"Valsartan","recallId":"D-0303-2019","classification":"II","reason":"CGMP Deviations: FDA laboratory testing confirmed presence of an impurity, N-nitrosodimethylamine (NDEA) in product.","status":"Ongoing","sourceUrl":"https://api.fda.gov/drug/enforcement.json?search=recall_number:%22D-0303-2019%22"}

{"patientId":"PX-005","chars":535,"bulletin":"⚠️ Pharmacovigilance alert (patient PX-005).\nYour Amlodipine/Valsartan 5/160 mg (Teva) has an active FDA recall: recall D-0303-2019, Class II, after laboratory testing confirmed the impurity NDEA. 120,394 bottles distributed across the U.S. and Puerto Rico were recalled. Affected lots: 23X017 through 23X024 (NDC 0093-7690-56 and 0093-7690-98). Check your bottle: if your lot is listed, your pharmacy can replace it. Do not stop treatment without talking to your doctor: keeping your blood pressure under control remains the priority."}
